title: Law on Accounting (Law on Corporate Accounts, their Audit and the Accounting Profession)_020708
title_en: Law on Accounting (Law on Corporate Accounts, their Audit and the Accounting Profession)_020708
short_name: Law on Accounting (Law on Corporate Accounts, their Audit...

provisions.title: This law shall determine the organization, management, and function of accounting system
provisions.content:
based on international accounting standards for enterprises either natural persons or legal entities
having independent profession in the Kingdom of Cambodia.

provisions.title: In this Law, unless the context or subject matters otherwise requires:
provisions.content:
“Accounting” means a system of identifying, recording, assembling, measuring, classifying, and

presentation of financial information concerning the activities of an entity.

“Audit” means the systematic review or examination of the assertions or actions of a business entity to
evaluate whether that entity is complied with the standards. Audit services include financial statement

audits, performance audits, compilation, reviews, and agreed upon procedures. Only a registered auditor

complying with this Law may perform these services and sign such reports as an auditor.

“Financial Reporting” means the reports containing information on the financial position, the results of

activities, and the cash flows of an accounting entity during a specific period.

“International Accounting Standards” means the standards promulgated by the
International Accounting Standards Board.

provisions.title: In compliance with current laws, all enterprises, whether natural or legal entities, are required
provisions.content:
to keep books and accounts and have them audited in accordance with the terms and conditions
provided for under this Law.

provisions.title: Enterprises shall prepare financial statements on a yearly basis that are in compliance with
provisions.content:
both the conceptual framework and Cambodian Accounting Standards, the principles of which are set

out by PRAKAS proclaimed by the Minister of the Ministry of Economy and Finance and in line with the
International Accounting Standards.

provisions.title: Enterprises shall keep their accounts in accounting books as defined by PRAKAS announced by
provisions.content:
the Minister of Economy and Finance, and in line with the International Accounting Standards. All

accounting transactions shall be supported by documentary evidence.

provisions.title: A National Accounting Council shall be established under the auspices of the Ministry of
provisions.content: Economy and Finance.

provisions.title: The National Accounting Council is thereby mandated to:
The financial statements shall include the balance sheet, the income statement, the cash flow
provisions.content:
statement and explanatory notes. They shall be considered as an integral part of the financial

statements.

provisions.title: The accounting records referred to in Article 12 shall be prepared in the Khmer language and
provisions.content:
expressed in Riels. Enterprises carrying out business with foreign countries or which are subsidiaries of

foreign companies may be authorised to prepare accounting records in English and/or in a currency

other than Riels along with the accounting records in the Khmer language and Khmer Riels in

compliance with the conditions set out by the Ministry
of Economy and Finance. However, the financial statements referred to in article 4 shall be prepared in

the Khmer language and in Riels.

provisions.title: The duration of the accounting period shall be twelve months. The accounting period shall
provisions.content:
begin on the first day of January and end on the 31st

day of December of the same year. As for the newly established enterprises, the annual financial

reporting for the first fiscal year could start from the date of its formation and end on the date of the
31st day of December

of the next year. Another date for fiscal year may otherwise be set for the activities of a specific

company.

provisions.title: The financial statements shall be prepared within three months following the closure of the
provisions.content:
financial year. In the event of an enterprise not being in a position to comply with this deadline, it shall

request authorisation from the Minister of Economy and Finance to close the accounts and to prepare
them on another dates.

provisions.title: The financial statements and the corresponding ledgers and documentary evidence shall be
provisions.content: kept for at least ten years. Such ledgers include a general journal, accounting ledger and inventory book.

provisions.title: The financial statements and the accounting ledgers provided for in article 12 may not be
provisions.content: legally recognized unless they are in compliance with the provisions of this Law.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Institute of Certified Public Accountants and Auditors
provisions.title: An Institute of Khmer Certified Public Accountants and Auditors shall be formed among
provisions.content:
accountancy professionals. The Institute shall operate under the auspices of the Ministry of Economy

and Finance.

The organization and functioning of the Institute, and the formulation of professional regulations for

Certified Public Accountants and Auditors, which are enrolled respectively on

two separate lists; shall be set out by Anu Kret.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4: Institute of Certified Public Accountants and Auditors
provisions.title: The Institute shall be specifically formed with a view to:
All enterprises, whether natural or legal entities, shall submit their accounts to be audited by
provisions.content:
an independent auditor in the event that their turnover, and/or balance sheet total, and/or number of

employees are above the limits set by PRAKAS of the Ministry of Economy and Finance. The audit shall

be carried out by a natural person or legal entity
enrolled on the list of registered auditors referred to in article 14 of this Law.

provisions.title: The practice of professional accountant shall be carried out by a natural person or legal
provisions.content:
entity enrolled on the list of Khmer professional accountants as referred to in article 14 of this Law. The

practice of professional accountants is in conflict of interest with those in the
civil service and political function in legislative, executive and the judiciary bodies.

provisions.title: All natural persons and directors of legally incorporated entities contravening to the
provisions.content:
provisions in articles 3, 4, and 5 shall be subjected to a fine between five and ten million Riels, and/or

may serve a prison term from one year to two years.

All natural persons and directors of legally incorporated entities who fail to prepare or file proper
financial statements in compliance with the provisions stipulated in this Law, shall be subjected to a fine

of between five and ten million Riels and/or may serve a prison term from three to six months.

In addition to the above penalties, natural persons or director(s) of legal entity shall be subject to the

revocation of licence(s), the details of which shall be set out by Anu Kret.

provisions.title: The professional accountants and auditors who fail to comply with the provisions of this Law
provisions.content:
shall be subjected to a fine of between ten to twenty million Riels.

Without considering the provision of revocation of licence(s), the fines shall be increased to twofold

and/or subjected to a prison term of between one year to three years, in the event of repeating

offenses.

provisions.title: Any provisions contrary to the Law are deemed null and void.
provisions.content:
Dated in Phnom Penh, this 8th day of July 2002

(signed)

Norodom Sihanouk

Submitted to The Signature of The King

The Prime Minister
(signed)

Hun Sen

Communicated to Samdech The Prime Minister

The Senior Minister and Minister of economy and Finance
(signed)

Keat Chhon
